differentials.name: Fetal growth restriction (38-77%) ← nutritional loss from exposed bowel

context: =  paramedian full-thickness abdominal fusion defect usually on right side of umbilical cord; may involve thorax; nonrotated bowel lacking secondary fixation to dorsal abdominal wall Prevalence:   4÷10,000 live births (in 2005), sporadic Cause:   deficient mesenchyme during lateral body wall folding former theory:  abnormal involution of right umbilical vein / premature interruption of right omphalomesenteric artery with ischemic damage to abdominal wall Risk factor:   young maternal age (teenage mother÷mother aged > 25 years = 7÷1); more common in non-Hispanic white mothers; NO sex predilecti
